summary: Extracted shared EmergencyCard component from EmergencyCardView. The new EmergencyCard.tsx accepts card: EmergencyCardData and optional resolvePhotoUrl, renders header through footer with no page-level wrapper. EmergencyCardView.tsx is now a thin shell: full-page background div wrapping <EmergencyCard />. EmergencyCardPreview.tsx was rewritten to accept pet: Pet (full object, replacing petName + specialInstructions) plus existing health arrays, builds an EmergencyCardData-shaped object from those arrays (filtering by show_on_card/is_active), then renders <EmergencyCard /> inside a 375px mobile device frame with rounded corners and shadow. Send Card / Edit buttons sit outside the frame. OverviewSection.tsx updated to pass pet={pet} instead of petName + specialInstructions. TypeScript compilation clean with zero errors.
notes_for_reviewer: EmergencyCardPreview now accepts pet: Pet instead of petName: string + specialInstructions: string | null. OverviewSection was updated accordingly.
The owner field in the preview card is set to null — the owner is the authenticated viewer, no need to show themselves as a contact in the preview.
generated_at in the preview uses new Date().toISOString() since it is a live preview, not a snapshot. The footer will show today's date.
buildAlerts and formatWeight are exported from EmergencyCard.tsx in case other consumers need them later.

The sticky header inside EmergencyCard uses position: sticky. Inside the overflow:hidden mobile frame in the preview it will not stick (overflow:hidden clips sticky positioning). This is acceptable for a preview frame — the card content is still fully visible.
